title: Clean and Safe Team Homeless Actions and Outcomes - March 2026
resolved_text:
1 
 
Memorandum 
DATE: April 22, 2026 
TO:  Mayor and City Council  
THROUGH: Jackie Bryant, City Manager 
FROM: Cynthia Esparza, Director 
Kaytlin Canner, Clean and Safe Manager 
DEPT:  Community Engagement and Services     
SUBJECT: Clean and Safe Team Homeless Actions and Outcomes – March 2026  
The City of Reno continues to address homeless-related concerns and provide outreach to the 
unsheltered community within city limits through the Clean and Safe Team. The Clean and Safe Team 
interdepartmental team plays a critical role in addressing the multifaceted challenges and 
community impacts associated with homelessness, while coordinating multiple concurrent efforts 
and programs to ensure a comprehensive, citywide response. 
Clean and Safe Team - Departments 
• Licensing and Code Enforcement  
• Clean and Safe, Reno Direct - 
Community Engagement and 
Services 
• Security - City Manager's Office 
• Housing and Neighborhood 
Development 
 
• Maintenance and Operations 
• Parks and Recreation  
• Community Action and Outreach 
(CAO), Mobile Engagement Team 
(MET), Parking Enforcement              
-Reno Police
The following is an overview of March 2026 data, actions, outcomes, and updates from the Clean and Safe Team: 
March 2026 Key Highlights 
• 6 community engagement and 
outreach efforts  
• 41 outreach interactions 
• 4 housing arrangements   
• 176 citations 
• 96 arrests 
• 3 large encampment clean-ups 
• 19 smaller encampment clean-ups 

2 
 
• 164 yards of trash collected 
• 50 occupied vehicles posted 
• 15 occupied vehicles towed 
• 6 park exclusions 
• 33 Code Enforcement cases 
• 138 Allied Security site visits
March Updates 
• Reno Housing Authority Motel Assistance Program: In March, Clean and Safe  
Outreach facilitated 1 individual into the Motel Assistance program. One individual 
successfully transitioned out of the Motel Assistance Program into permanent housing.  
• Camera Mobile Trailers: Clean and Safe's Mobile Camera Trailer Pilot Program is 
ongoing. The mobile camera trailers continue to keep previously chronic areas clear and serve as 
a deterrent. The mobile camera trailers are currently installed at Spokane Street, along the railroad and 
Galletti Way, along the railroad. 
Community Engagement and Partnerships  
Multiple departments partake in standing or topic specific meetings with regional partners to 
help address homelessness, identify support, and advance collaborative, solution-oriented 
initiatives. March highlights are as follows:

Outreach Interactions  
Location Individual 
Interactions 
Service 
Acceptance Outcomes 
Phone Call 1 0 
Clean and Safe Outreach spoke with a father 
who was seeking motel assistance for 
the father, the father's daughter, and the father's granddaughter. Our 
Place was suggested and the father agreed to 
explore Our Place as an option. After connecting with 
Our Place, Our Place staff confirmed the daughter and 
granddaughter are on the Our Place waitlist. 
Clean and Safe followed up with the father 
to inquire if the father, daughter, and granddaughter are interested in reuniting 
with family in Sacramento, as the father's daughter 
has a plan moving forward. The father declined 
reunification and plans to stay with the father's daughter 
until the daughter and granddaughter move into Our Place.  
EconoLodge 2 2 
Clean and Safe Outreach transported a 
couple's personal belongings from Siegel 
Suites - Nevadan to EconoLodge, as the couple did 
not qualify for Siegel Suites - Nevadan. The 
couple was housed through the Reno 
Housing Authority Motel Assistance 
Program, while the couple awaits permanent 
housing in April. 
Siegel Suites - 
Nevadan 1 1 
Clean and Safe Outreach connected with a 
client enrolled in the Reno Housing 
Authority Motel Assistance Program to 
discuss housing options. During the check-
in, the client shared the client's medical concerns. 
Plan is to have UNR StreetMed Team follow 
up to treat the client's wounds.

fields.authors: Cynthia Esparza, Director; Kaytlin Canner, Clean and Safe Manager
fields.tldr: The Clean and Safe Team's March 2026 report summarizes interdepartmental efforts to address homelessness in Reno, including 41 outreach interactions, 4 housing arrangements, 96 arrests, and 22 encampment clean-ups. Service requests totaled 351, a 9% increase over February, driven largely by a 35% rise in Occupied Vehicle Nuisance reports. Outreach facilitated motel assistance placements, Safe Camp referrals, and coordination with regional partners. Mobile camera trailers continue to deter chronic encampment activity at key locations.
fields.key_stats: 41 outreach interactions; 4 housing arrangements; 1 shelter arrangement; 176 citations; 96 arrests; 3 large and 19 smaller encampment clean-ups; 164 yards of trash collected; 50 occupied vehicles posted; 15 occupied vehicles towed; 6 park exclusions; 33 Code Enforcement cases; 138 Allied Security site visits; 351 total Reno Direct service requests (9% increase over February); Occupied Vehicle Nuisance requests up 35% (148 to 200); Camping in City Parks down 47% (17 to 9)
fields.key_decisions: Mobile Camera Trailer Pilot Program remains ongoing at Spokane Street and Galletti Way; 1 individual facilitated into Reno Housing Authority Motel Assistance Program; 1 individual transitioned from Motel Assistance Program into permanent housing; 2 individuals referred to and approved for Safe Camp; collaboration initiated with University of Nevada Reno Police Department regarding problem areas surrounding the university
fields.action_items: UNR StreetMed Team to follow up and treat wounds of Siegel Suites - Nevadan client enrolled in Reno Housing Authority Motel Assistance Program; continued monitoring of Our Place waitlist status for daughter and granddaughter; ongoing mobile camera trailer deployment at Spokane Street and Galletti Way locations
